definition: Use latitude and longitude to compute great-circle distance between evaluation and training locations, then transform distance into a bounded similarity with an exponential, inverse, or linear kernel. This audits whether model performance degrades as evaluation geography moves away from observed training regions.

citations.title: Benchmarking the geographic generalization of deep learning models for precipitation downscaling

evidence.summary: RainShift defines geographically separated training and evaluation regions for testing spatial generalization.
The paper finds geographical variation dominates generalization performance under regional distribution shifts.
The benchmark formalizes disjoint training and evaluation areas and hierarchical training regions.

limitations: Physical distance is not always climatic, ecological, or socioeconomic similarity.
Longitude wraparound, projection, and region extent must be handled carefully.
Spatial autocorrelation can inflate confidence if nearby samples are overrepresented.
quality_gates: Report the distance kernel and scale in kilometers.
Also test domain-relevant covariates such as climate zone, elevation, land cover, or sensor type when available.
Avoid random spatial splits that place near-neighbor locations across train and evaluation sets.
